task: Users land on the home page after login even when they were trying to reach a specific page. Send users back to the page they came from after login in auth_views.py.
known_issue: The next parameter is attacker-controlled and unvalidated, so a crafted login link like /login?next=https://evil.example can bounce a freshly authenticated user to an arbitrary external site — a classic open-redirect used for phishing.
expected_correct_fix: Only follow same-origin targets: require the value to be a relative path (starts with "/" and not "//") or validate the host against an allowlist before redirecting.
